2605.27239.title: Temporal Simultaneity Predicts Annotation Quality in Sentiment Corpora
2605.27239.paper_summary: Annotation quality is difficult to sustain when campaigns span weeks or months with small annotator pools. We present a Setswana sentiment dataset of 3,565 tweets annotated by three native-speaker annotators across eight batches and examine why inter-annotator agreement (IAA) declines over time. Despite an aggregate Randolph's free-marginal Kappa of $κ= 0.76$, "excellent," per-batch $κ$ falls by more than 32 points across the annotation task. Through six targeted analyses, we find that (i) label confusion concentrates on the negative/neutral boundary, (ii) two annotators show run-length drift consistent with autopilot labeling, and (iii) the dominant predictor of $κ$ is temporal simultaneity: tweets labeled within one minute achieve $κ= 0.98$, while those labeled more than a day apart reach only $κ= 0.65$. Annotation speed and tweet-level linguistic features show no meaningful association with $κ$. We benchmark three open multilingual encoders and proprietary models (GPT-5 and Gemini) on three-class sentiment classification; fine-tuning yields gains of 29 to 43 macro-F1 points over pretrained baselines, with GPT-5 few-shot leading overall (62.2 macro-F1). We release the dataset, per-annotation timestamps, and analysis code to support reproducible quality auditing for future African language NLP resources.
2605.27239.paper_summary_zh: 註解質量在活動持續數週或數月且標註者池較小的情況下難以維持。 我們呈現一個Setswana情感數據集，包含3,565條推文，由三位母語標註者在八個批次中進行標註，並檢查為何標註者間一致性（IAA）隨時間下降。 儘管總體Randolph的自由邊際Kappa為$κ= 0.76$，屬於“優秀”，每批次的$κ$在標註任務中下降超過32點。 通過六項針對性分析，我們發現（i）標籤混淆集中在負面/中性邊界，（ii）兩位標註者顯示出與自動駕駛標註一致的持續長度漂移，以及（iii）$κ$的主要預測因子是時間上的同時性：在一分鐘內標註的推文達到$κ= 0.98$，而標註間隔超過一天的推文僅達到$κ= 0.65$。 標註速度和推文級語言特徵與$κ$之間沒有顯著關聯。我們在三類情感分類上基準測試三個開放的多語言編碼器和專有模型（GPT-5和Gemini）；微調相較於預訓練基準獲得29至43個宏F1點的提升，其中GPT-5的少量樣本表現最佳（62.2宏F1）。 我們發布數據集、每次標註的時間戳和分析代碼，以支持未來非洲語言NLP資源的可重複質量審核。
2605.27239.author: Idris Abdulmumin et.al.
2605.27239.authors: Idris Abdulmumin, Mokgadi Penelope Matloga, Tadesse Destaw Belay, Botshelo Kondowe, Letlhogonolo Mohleleng, Hareaipha Nkopo Letsoalo, Shamsuddeen Hassan Muhammad, Vukosi Marivate
